enforced.oracle: The canonical JSON representation of the parsed VCF must be identical regardless of the order of meta-information lines (except ##fileformat must remain first).

evidence.quote: Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.

enforced.mr_name: Structured meta-line key-value pair ordering invariance
enforced.oracle: The canonical JSON representation of the parsed VCF must be identical regardless of the order of key=value pairs within structured ##INFO/##FORMAT/##FILTER meta-lines.

evidence.quote: Implementations must not rely on the order of the fields within structured lines and are not required to preserve field ordering.

enforced.mr_name: ALT allele permutation with consistent GT and Number=A/R field remapping
enforced.oracle: The parsed variant semantics (allele identities, genotype calls, and per-allele INFO values) must be identical after permutation, with GT indices and Number=A/R fields consistently remapped according to the permutation.

evidence.quote:
* R: The field has one value for each possible allele, including the reference.

A: The field has one value per alternate allele.
  The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column (described in section data-lines).

enforced.mr_name: Fileformat must be first line rejection

evidence.quote: A single `fileformat' line is always required, must be the first line in the file, and details the VCF format version number.
Meta-information lines can be in any order, with the exception of ##fileformat which must come first.

evidence.quote:
If genotype information is present, then the same types of data must be present for all samples.
First a FORMAT field is given specifying the data types and order (colon-separated FORMAT keys matching the regular expression \^[A-Za-z\_][0-9A-Za-z\_.]*\$, duplicate keys are not allowed).
This is followed by one data block per sample, with the colon-separated data corresponding to the types specified in the format.

preconditions: Input is a valid VCF file with INFO and FORMAT fields defined

evidence.quote: INFO — additional information: Semicolon-separated series of additional information fields, or the MISSING value `.' if none are present.

quarantine.mr_name: Multi-allelic split invariance with synchronized GT and per-allele fields
quarantine.oracle: A multi-ALT record split into separate single-ALT records must represent the same variant set, with GT indices remapped to preserve REF (0) semantics and Number=A/R fields correctly distributed across split records

evidence.quote: * ALT — alternate base(s): Comma-separated list of alternate non-reference alleles.
* ALT — alternate base(s): Comma-separated list of alternate non-reference alleles.

* R: The field has one value for each possible allele, including the reference.

quarantine.oracle: Trimming shared prefix/suffix bases between REF and ALT to produce left-anchored parsimonious representation must yield the same canonical variant per Tan, Abecasis, Kang 2015 normalization

evidence.chunk_id: VCFv4.5.tex::Understanding the VCF format and the haplotype representation::p580
evidence.quote:
VCF records use a simple haplotype representation for REF and ALT alleles to describe variant haplotypes at a locus.
ALT haplotypes are constructed from the REF haplotype by taking the REF allele bases at the POS in the reference genotype and replacing them with the ALT bases.
In essence, the VCF record specifies a-REF-t and the alternative haplotypes are a-ALT-t for each alternative allele.
evidence.section_id: Understanding the VCF format and the haplotype representation

quarantine.oracle: Left-shifting an indel within a homopolymer run (where REF[0]==REF[-1]) must produce an equivalent canonical representation per Tan 2015 §2.1 normalization rules
